entry.title: A Week of Smoky Skies Across North America - NASA Science
entry.summary: Brown carbon, emitted by wildland fires and tracked over the span of a week in July 2026, worsened air quality in parts of the U.S. and Canada.

entry.title: Single-player games deserve more attention in esports
entry.summary: League of Legends and Counter-Strike 2 seem to get all the love, but single-player games deserve way more attention.

entry.title: NISAR’s L-Band Radar Reveals ‘Hummingbird’ in Antarctica - NASA Science
entry.summary: Data from the Earth-orbiting U.S.-India NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) satellite’s L-band radar was used to produce an image of Nunatak

entry.title: Ruthenium nanoparticles convert captured perchlorate into harmless chloride in water treatment waste
entry.summary: For decades, water utilities have relied on giant tanks filled with ion-exchange resin beads to remove perchlorate, a harmful industrial pollutant, from drinking water. The system works by attracting negatively charged perchlorate ions to positively charged resin beads, allowing purified water to flow out of the tanks. But the process leaves behind a difficult problem: resin beads loaded with perchlorate that must be regenerated or disposed of as hazardous waste.

entry.title: Built in Fort Worth: Wistron Opens Advanced Manufacturing Plant to Produce NVIDIA AI Systems
entry.summary: The AI era runs on AI infrastructure. Many of these advanced systems are built and tested in Texas. Wistron opened its first U.S. manufacturing facility today in Fort Worth — a 324,000-square-foot greenfield plant producing superchips at the heart of some of the world’s most capable AI systems. In front of an audience of Wistron […]

entry.title: What do people in middle-income countries think about cuts to U.S. humanitarian aid?
entry.summary: In 16 middle-income countries surveyed, many people are unsure how U.S. humanitarian aid cuts will affect them, though those with opinions lean negative.

entry.title: U.S. foreign aid is plunging in second Trump administration
entry.summary: U.S. foreign aid fell to its lowest inflation-adjusted level in over two decades in fiscal 2025, dropping to $47.3 billion — more than $24 billion less than the prior year.

entry.title: Overwatch reveals Shion's original face and Hashimoto elders in new story
entry.summary: Overwatch's new Shion short story titled "Ambush" revealed Shion's original omnic form plus the names and personalities of the Hashimoto elders.

entry.summary: Proposing a new metric for whether AI does what you actually want

entry.title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber
entry.summary: Google introduces Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber to help defenders find, validate, and patch software vulnerabilities quickly and efficiently.

entry.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Driving Performance Per Watt, Lowest Token Cost for Partners Worldwide
entry.summary: Backed by 300 global partners, Vera Rubin is ramping up worldwide. NVIDIA partners CoreWeave, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure and Mistral are among many deploying Vera Rubin, which delivers benchmark leadership on performance per watt and lowest token costs.

entry.title: MLBB backtracks after fans outrage over AI-generated main menu in advanced server
entry.summary: Fans are upset about the recent MLBB Advanced Server update that adds a 3D model of Layla that seems to be generated by AI.

entry.title: Saudia unveils esports-branded plane for EWC 2026's Paris debut
entry.summary: Saudia's expanded deal with the Esports Foundation includes a specially branded plane flying daily between Riyadh and Paris for the duration of EWC 2026.

entry.title: A New Compact Instrument Enables High-Fidelity Measurements of Energetic Particles on CubeSats - NASA Science
entry.summary: “Most CubeSats can observe particles from only a single direction, so they have to spin in order to build up a full picture — and that takes a few seconds,

entry.title: Broad Institute, Boston Children’s Hospital, and The Jackson Laboratory launch the Center for Therapeutic Genetics, a non-profit effort to develop genetic medicines for rare and ultra-rare diseases
entry.summary: The new collaboration aims to develop precision medicines, including base and prime editing, to treat patients with rare diseases, using infrastructure and repeatable practices the center will share with others. div.hero-section__author { display:none;}

entry.title: BLG Bin to take a temporary break amid health concerns
entry.summary: Due to health concerns, BLG top laner Bin will be taking a break from competitive League of Legends ahead of LPL Split 3.

entry.title: Team Vitality signs BYD as official vehicle partner for summer 2026
entry.summary: BYD France and Team Vitality have confirmed a new vehicle supply deal covering rosters across Rocket League, Tekken 8, Teamfight Tactics, and several other titles for the summer period.

entry.title: Exclusive: Medical student in Nepal behind busy research factory
entry.summary: A second-year medical student in Nepal is at the center of an international research network churning out everything from systematic reviews and meta-analyses to case reports and database studies, …

entry.title: Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves Season 3 has six new fighters — and a character identity crisis
entry.summary: SNK unveiled a relentless Season 3 schedule for Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves, but a growing chorus of competitive players argues that more characters won't fix the game's "sauce" problem.

entry.title: New Overwatch Lucio skin enters Heroes of the Storm!
entry.summary: The Ruby Chromesthesia Lucio skin can now be unlocked in Heroes of the Storm. The patch also introduces the mighty Slapathur ability!

entry.summary: Our monetary policy strategy, the tools we use and the impact they have

entry.title: Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves has an insane DLC schedule, but it may not be enough to bring the sauce
entry.summary: Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves devs are going ham with the DLC, dropping tons of characters. But do they have the sauce?

entry.title: Molecules stop carbon nanotubes clumping, unlocking record heat-to-electricity performance
entry.summary: QUT researchers have overcome a challenge that has limited next-generation energy-harvesting materials for more than two decades, opening the door to more powerful wearable electronics and new ways of turning wasted heat into electricity. The breakthrough centers on carbon nanotubes, which are flexible, conductive microscopic rods that have long shown promise for wearable technologies but have been difficult to control.

entry.title: Carbon nanotube model reveals how smart sensors perform at aircraft-like extremes
entry.summary: Researchers from Skoltech, in collaboration with the Harbin Institute of Technology and Jiangsu University (China), have published a study presenting the first empirical model to explain and predict the sensing behavior of hierarchical tri-phase carbon nanotube systems across a wide temperature range (from -170°C (-274°F) to 90°C (194°F)). The study has been published in the Carbon journal.

entry.title: NASA-Funded Study Shows How Controlled Burns Help Sequoias Survive Wildfires - NASA Science
entry.summary: A new study, funded by NASA, found that giant sequoias were nearly four times more likely to survive extreme wildfires in forests that have been managed with prescribed burns, which decrease tree mortality.

entry.title: Acid-resistant nanocage shows promise for targeted gastric cancer therapy
entry.summary: Researchers from the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, National University of Singapore (NUS Medicine), have developed a swallowable nanoscale delivery platform designed to transport therapeutic enzymes through the stomach's acidic environment and activate a cancer-killing reaction at tumor sites. Early preclinical findings suggest that the approach may offer a new strategy for treating gastric cancer more precisely while reducing damage to healthy tissue.

entry.title: Fortnite's latest Sprite trailer features an 'Obsession' crossover – could skins be next?
entry.summary: Fortnite skins based on the film Obsession have become a real possibility following a recent collaboration with the film!

entry.title: IEEE Program Helps Girls In India See a Future In STEM
entry.summary: Hands-on projects and role models open doors for STEM careers

entry.title: Team Vitality Celebrates MWI at EWC 2026 win by eating giant croissants
entry.summary: Team Vitality wins a back-to-back MWI at EWC 2026 championship title after defeating NAVI PH in a six-round series.

entry.title: South American Dota 2 just got dealt one of its worst blows yet after PlayTime's scandal at the Esports World Cup
entry.summary: South American Dota 2 just got dealt one of its worst blows in God knows how long after PlayTime's EWC elimination.

entry.title: How global energy production is changing, in 6 charts
entry.summary: Fossil fuels still dominate global electricity, but wind and solar are surging. In 2025, renewables outpaced coal worldwide for the first time, supplying 34% of electricity.

entry.title: NVIDIA NVLink: The Scale-Up Network for AI Factories | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: The demand for AI continues to accelerate. Workloads are getting larger, models are becoming more complex, and there is mounting pressure to deploy AI compute infrastructure faster than ever.

entry.title: At SIGGRAPH, NVIDIA Advances Graphics and Simulation With Agentic and Physical AI
entry.summary: From open models to real-time simulation, AI and graphics breakthroughs are transforming media, content creation and robotics.

entry.title: Integrate NVIDIA Omniverse RTX Sensor Simulation Into Existing Apps | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: Developers building 3D, design, simulation, robotics, and industrial digital twin applications need ways to bring physical AI capabilities into the tools and services they already use.

entry.title: Towards ODRL 3.0: IPTC's RightsML at W3C ODRL Workshop - IPTC
entry.summary: IPTC is the global standards body of the news media. We provide the technical foundation for the news ecosystem.

entry.title: Microsoft expands Azure AI and HPC infrastructure with AMD - The Official Microsoft Blog
entry.summary: AI workloads are scaling faster than any single infrastructure approach can support — with more models, new agent-driven workloads and surging compute demand driving the need for greater specialization across the stack. To meet this need, Microsoft continues to evolve Azure’s infrastructure, including expanding its AI fleet with AMD’s most advanced AI and high-performance computing...

entry.title: We’re Squandering LEDs’ Potential to Save Our Night Skies
entry.summary: Bad decisions have turned efficient technology into a driver of light pollution. There is a better way

entry.title: Beyond the Score: Why “Explain Your Thinking” Matters
entry.summary: Learn how Explain Your Thinking helps educators use AI to make student reasoning visible and improve feedback, assessment, and intervention.

entry.title: Building Europe’s geological future - British Geological Survey
entry.summary: BGS scientists are contributing to a continent-wide initiative that is strengthening collaboration across borders to tackle shared subsurface challenges and unlock new opportunities.

entry.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Building Life Science Industry’s Most Advanced AI Factory on NVIDIA Vera Rubin
entry.summary: The pharmaceutical company has demonstrated how AI can transform how drugs discovery and is now scaling AI across R&D to accelerate the discovery of next-generation medicines.
